stemHtml: Which flap setting should be used for landing if a trimmable horizontal stabiliser becomes jammed during cruise flight and why?

options.a: Full flaps, as all aircraft are certified to land with full flaps in any trim position.
options.b: An intermediate flap setting, as this allows for greater pitch authority during approach and landing.
options.c: An intermediate flap setting, as extending full flaps will not be possible due to the airspeed.
options.d: Full flaps, as this will give better control authority in the event of a go-around.
